Choropleth map of appointment acceptance rates by US state

Source: R/map_acceptance_rate.R
mysterycall_map_acceptance_rate.Rd

Draws a choropleth map of appointment acceptance rates using base-R map data (no external spatial packages required for state-level maps).

Usage

mysterycall_map_acceptance_rate(
  data,
  region_col,
  rate_col,
  region_type = c("state", "hrr"),
  palette = "viridis",
  title = "Appointment Acceptance Rate (%)",
  legend_title = "Acceptance\nRate (%)",
  limits = NULL,
  na_color = "grey80",
  save_path = NULL,
  width = 10,
  height = 7,
  dpi = 300L
)

Arguments

data

A data frame with at least two columns: region_col and rate_col.

region_col

Character scalar. Name of the column that identifies regions. For region_type = "state" this can be full state names (e.g. "Colorado") or standard two-letter abbreviations (e.g. "CO").

rate_col

Character scalar. Name of a numeric column with acceptance rates between 0 and 1. Values are multiplied by 100 for display.

region_type

Character scalar. Either "state" (default) or "hrr". "hrr" immediately raises an error with instructions to use the sf-based workflow.

palette

Character scalar. Viridis colour-scale option passed to ggplot2::scale_fill_viridis_c(). Default "viridis".

title

Character scalar. Plot title. Default "Appointment Acceptance Rate (%)".

legend_title

Character scalar. Legend title. Default "Acceptance\\nRate (%)".

limits

Numeric vector of length 2 or NULL. Explicit limits for the fill scale. NULL (default) lets ggplot2 choose.

na_color

Character scalar. Fill colour for states with no data. Default "grey80".

save_path

Character scalar or NULL. When not NULL, the plot is written to this path via mysterycall_save_plot().

width

Numeric. Width in inches passed to mysterycall_save_plot(). Default 10.

height

Numeric. Height in inches passed to mysterycall_save_plot(). Default 7.

dpi

Integer. Resolution passed to mysterycall_save_plot(). Default 300L.

Value

The ggplot object, invisibly.

See also

Other mapping: mysterycall_clear_isochrone_cache(), mysterycall_create_isochrones(), mysterycall_isochrones_for_df(), mysterycall_map_acog_districts(), mysterycall_map_base(), mysterycall_map_block_group(), mysterycall_map_leaflet(), mysterycall_map_physicians(), mysterycall_plot_density(), mysterycall_plot_isochrones(), mysterycall_plot_line(), mysterycall_plot_scatter()

Examples

set.seed(7)
df <- data.frame(
  state = c("Colorado", "California", "Texas", "New York", "Florida"),
  rate  = c(0.55, 0.72, 0.48, 0.63, 0.81)
)
mysterycall_map_acceptance_rate(df, region_col = "state", rate_col = "rate")
#> Error in ggplot2::map_data("state"): The package "maps" is required for `map_data()`.
